I purchased an Ola S1x 2nd Generation in June 2025. After one month of use, the hub motor and MCU failed. Despite waiting around 9 months for replacement parts, the newly installed hub motor and MCU also became defective within 1–2 days. When I reported the issue, I received an unsatisfactory response from the service centre staff, and my scooter is still lying at the service centre without resolution. I request immediate action to replace the defective components and resolve this issues.

After owning three OLA Electric scooters for over three years, I can confidently say this has been our worst vehicle ownership experience. Selling scooters seems easy, but after-sales service and customer support have been terrible in our experience. Issues remain unresolved, service is frustrating, and customers are left helpless. The company appears completely indifferent to customer problems. I would never buy from them again and would not recommend it to anyone.

Too many banners and ads pushing Ola scooters/bikes to existing customers. Vehicles aren't mobile phones that people replace every year. What's the point of repeatedly advertising the same products to current owners? Instead, please fix the app. It often shows the vehicle as "sleeping", fails to connect, and makes raising complaints difficult. MoveOS customers pay for software features and reliability, not endless upselling. Focus on improving the ownership experience.

The UI is not good, it wasn't designed to be optimal with the user in question. Overall, the concept is good, but the app needs to be more stable, reliable, and user-friendly.

This supposed to be an essential app that unlocks the scooter. The landing page should be kept as light as possible so users can access the unlock option quickly.
